zh.lifeSummary: 當James fitzjohn Barry於1485年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克科克時，他的父親Gerald Fitzgerald Roe30歲，母親Lady Miss O'Driscoll Roe10歲。他於1500年與Ellen Reardan在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克科克科克 1結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1557年3月20日在愛爾蘭享年72歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James fitzjohn Barry于1485年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 科克科克时，他的父亲Gerald Fitzgerald Roe30岁，母亲Lady Miss O'Driscoll Roe10岁。他于1500年与Ellen Reardan在爱尔兰庫迪 科克科克科克 1结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1557年3月20日在爱尔兰享年72岁。

nameMeanings.name: Barry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donal, Aidan, Ciaran, Eoin, John Patrick, Kaitlin, Kieran, Murphy, Aileen, Brigid, Caitlin.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Beargha ‘descendant of Beargh’, a byname meaning ‘plunderer’.

Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Báire ‘descendant of Báire’, a short form of either of two Gaelic personal names, Bairrfhionn or Fionnbharr, both meaning ‘fair-topped’. This may also be the source of the Manx name Barry, whose family's main seat was Ballavarry in Kirk Andreas.

variants.name: Barr
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow) and northern Irish (Ulster): habitational name from any of various places in southwestern Scotland, in particular Ayrshire and Renfrewshire, named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’ or a Brittonic cognate of this.

English and Welsh: habitational name from Great Barr in Staffordshire. This is from Brittonic barro- ‘top, summit’, referring to Barr Beacon; there is evidence that this was known as la Bare in the 13th century.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Barre-en-Ouche in Eure, France, or perhaps from Barre-de-Semilly in Manche, France.

variants.name: Barrie
meanings: Scottish (Angus): habitational name from Barry in Angus or from another place so named, generally named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’, or from a British cognate of this word. This name is also the Scottish form of Barry .
French (Barrié): variant of Barrier .
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

variants.name: Berry
meanings: Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Béara ‘descendant of Béara’, a personal name of unexplained etymology; or, in some cases, perhaps an Anglicized form of Irish and Manx Ó Beargha. Compare Barry 1.
Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Barrie  .
English: habitational name from any of several places called in Devon named with Old English byrig, dative case of burh ‘fortified manor house, stronghold’, such as Berry Pomeroy and Berrynarbor.

variants.name: Dubarry
meanings: French: variant of the topographic or habitational name Barry , with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’.

variants.name: Baril
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Normand, Alain, Armand, Ghislaine, Jacques, Jean-Marc, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Monique, Phillippe.
French: from Old French baril ‘barrel’, applied as a metonymic occupational name for a cooper, or a nickname for a rotund man. Compare Barry 7 and Berry 7.

History: Jean Baril from the Saintonge region of France was in QC by 1673, when he married Marie Guillet, the first of his three wives. He died in 1724 in Batiscan, QC. His descendants also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Barrie, Bar(r)y, and Berry.

variants.name: Bary
meanings: Walloon and northern French: variant of   Barry   8.
French (southern): rare variant of   Barry   6.
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

nameMeanings.name: fitzjohn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name John , ‘son of John’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: Either a short form of any of the numerous names representing a transferred use of a surname formed with the Anglo-Norman French prefix fi(t)z ‘son of ’, as for example Fitzgerald , Fitzroy , or a transferred use of the surname Fitz, which may be of the same derivation, but applied originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same given name, or a local name from Fitz in Shropshire, named with the Old English personal name Fitt + hōh ‘hill spur’.

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: Fidgeon
meanings: 1  English: (i) variant of   Vivian  .  Compare  Videan  . (ii) variant of   Fitzjohn  .
2 Irish: reduced variant of Mag Guagáin, see McGuigan .

historicEvents.description: Henry VIII dies, succeeded by the boy king Edward VI. England and Ireland were ruled by the senior nobility of England.

en.lifeSummary: When James fitzjohn Barry was born in 1485, in Cork, County Cork, Ireland, his father, Gerald Fitzgerald Roe, was 30 and his mother, Lady Miss O'Driscoll Roe, was 10. He married Ellen Reardan in 1500, in Cork 1, Cork, County Cork, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 20 March 1557, in Ireland, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James fitzjohn Barry est né en 1485, à Cork, County Cork, Irlande, son père, Gerald Fitzgerald Roe, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lady Miss O'Driscoll Roe, avait 10 ans. Il a épousé Ellen Reardan en 1500, à Cork 1, Cork, County Cork, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 20 mars 1557, à Irlande, à l’âge de 72 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als James fitzjohn Barry 1485 in Cork, County Cork, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gerald Fitzgerald Roe 30 und seine Mutter Lady Miss O'Driscoll Roe 10. Er hat 1500 in Cork 1, Cork, County Cork, Irland Ellen Reardan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 20. März 1557 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James fitzjohn Barry è nato il nel 1485 nel Cork, County Cork, Irlanda, il suo padre, Gerald Fitzgerald Roe, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Lady Miss O'Driscoll Roe, aveva 10 anni. Lui sposato Ellen Reardan nel 1500, nel Cork 1, Cork, County Cork, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 20 marzo 1557, nel Irlanda, all'età di 72 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: James fitzjohn Barry(이)가 1485년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 코르크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gerald Fitzgerald Roe(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Miss O'Driscoll Roe(은)는 10세였다. 그는 1500년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 코르크 코르크 1에서 Ellen Reardan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1557년 3월 20일 아일랜드에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James fitzjohn Barry nasceu em 1485, em Cork, County Cork, Irlanda, seu pai, Gerald Fitzgerald Roe, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Lady Miss O'Driscoll Roe, tinha 10 anos. Ele casou-se com Ellen Reardan em 1500, em Cork 1, Cork, County Cork, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 20 de março de 1557, em Irlanda, com 72 anos.

Он женился на женщине по имени Ellen Reardan в 1500 г. в местности корк 1, корк, коунти корк, Ирландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 20 марта 1557 г. в местности Ирландия в возрасте 72 лет.
